rtl_config_fields.allowed: List of allowed values for the parameter (required). Sweepability is DERIVED from this field (run_lint --sweep and -rtl_sweep share bin/rtl_sweep_configs.py): a finite integer list is swept; an explicit empty list [] marks a free-valued parameter (no finite enumeration, e.g. a 32-bit MVENDORID) that skips the default-membership check, is fixed at 'default' and is never swept. There is no per-parameter sweep flag

build_config_fields.location: Lives under 'toolchain.build_config' alongside the toolchain profile-selection knobs (active, profiles). Each entry is a firmware-build axis: optimization level, libc, .rodata layout, etc.
build_config_fields.default: Default value (required). Strings -- these are firmware-build knobs forwarded as env vars into Makefiles and Embench's board.cfg
build_config_fields.allowed: List of allowed string values (required). Unlike rtl_config, these are not auto-swept; the integrator picks the variant by editing the 'default' field by hand
build_config_fields.env_var_name: Convention: BUILD_<KEY>. Exception: OPTIMIZATION keeps the legacy TC_OPT name for backward compatibility with the existing Makefiles. Per-test 'optimization' override (in the 'tests' array) still takes precedence over the build_config default.

test_fields.name: Test name (required)
test_fields.mode: Test instruction mode support: STD, COMP, or BOTH
test_fields.toolchain: Optional toolchain profile override (e.g., 'xpacks', 'gcc', 'clang'). Must match a key in toolchain.profiles. Defaults to toolchain.active.  Note: toolchain profiles support: 'unsupported_ext' list of extension names (e.g., ['zcmt']) stripped from -march; 'sysroot' set to 'auto' to auto-detect sysroot from GCC toolchain (useful for Clang using GCC's newlib headers/libs)
test_fields.optimization: Optional compiler optimization override (e.g., '-O3', '-Os'). Defaults to toolchain.optimization
test_fields.no_random_irq: Optional boolean flag to disable random IRQ injection for this test (default: false). Set to true for tests that manage their own trap handlers (e.g., trap_*, ecall, ebreak, wfi)
test_fields.no_variants: Optional boolean flag to skip timing variants in -all mode (default: false). Set to true for directed pipeline-timing tests that require exact back-to-back instruction execution
test_fields.is_benchmark: Optional boolean flag indicating this is a benchmark test
test_fields.score_pattern: Optional regex pattern to extract score from log (first capture group)
test_fields.score_metric: Optional metric name for the score (e.g., 'CoreMark/MHz', 'Dhrystones/sec')

SINGLE_CYCLE_BRANCH.description: Taken-branch latency: 1=zero-bubble (max IPC, lower Fmax), 0=one-bubble (lower IPC, max Fmax)

MUL_TYPE.description: Multiplier type: 1=1xCycle,  2=4xCycles,  3=16xCycles (valid only with Zmmul and M-extension)

MVENDORID.description: JEDEC manufacturer ID of the chip vendor integrator.

OPTIMIZATION.description: Compiler optimization level. Emitted as TC_OPT env var (legacy name preserved for Makefile compatibility). Per-test overrides via the 'optimization' field in a test entry still take precedence.

LIBC.description: C library: 'newlib' (default, full newlib) or 'newlib-nano' (--specs=nano.specs; smaller printf/malloc/softfloat)

RODATA_LOCATION.description: Read-only data placement: 'ROM' (default; .rodata next to .text) or 'SRAM' (mirrored to SRAM at boot via dedicated crt0+linker script)

modes.BOTH: Can run in both standard and compressed modes

tests.description: PLIC PRIV  - S-mode access to ctx-0 PLIC regs AHB-ERRORs -> load/store access fault (cause 5/7); ctx-1 still allowed

tests.name: trap_irq_plic_wfi_wake
tests.description: PLIC WFI   - PLIC source rising wakes the core out of WFI sleep -> MEI trap fires after wake

tests.name: trap_irq_plic_drain
tests.description: PLIC DRAIN - 4 sources asserted together, PLIC drains them in strict descending priority order

tests.name: trap_irq_aclint_msip
tests.description: ACLINT MSIP- ahb_aclint MSWI: firmware writes MSIP[0]=1 -> MSI trap (cause 3) -> handler clears MSIP[0]

tests.name: trap_irq_aclint_mtimer
tests.description: ACLINT MTI - ahb_aclint MTIMER: firmware programs MTIMECMP=mtime+0x80 -> MTI trap (cause 7) -> handler parks MTIMECMP=all-ones

tests.name: trap_irq_aclint_wfi_wake
tests.description: ACLINT WFI - ahb_aclint MTIMER WFI sleep wake: CPU enters WFI -> LF mtimecmp expiry un-gates hclk via wake aggregator -> MTI trap fires

tests.name: inst_zicntr_aclint_time
tests.description: ACLINT TIME- Zicntr time CSR routed through ahb_aclint Zicntr port: two reads non-zero and strictly increasing

tests.name: trap_irq_aclint_setssip
tests.description: ACLINT SSI - ahb_aclint SSWI SETSSIP: M-mode SETSSIP[0]=1 -> 1-cycle pulse -> MIP.SSIP -> M-mode SSI trap (cause 1) -> handler clears MIP.SSIP

tests.name: trap_irq_plic_size_violation
tests.description: PLIC SIZE  - sub-word (SB / LH) access to PLIC AHB-ERRORs -> load/store access fault (cause 5/7); spec mandates LW/SW only

tests.name: trap_irq_timing
tests.description: IRQ TIME   - Interrupt Timing Edge Cases (MRET re-entry, CSR race, WFI wakeup)

tests.name: trap_irq_inst_edge
tests.description: IRQ EDGE   - IRQ During Compressed/CSR/Branch Instructions

tests.name: trap_irq_kill_muldiv
tests.description: IRQ KILL   - IRQ Abort/Restart of Multi-Cycle MUL/DIV Operations

tests.name: trap_irq_kill_zcmp
tests.description: IRQ KILL   - IRQ Abort/Restart of Zcmp Push/Pop/Popret UOP Sequences

tests.name: trap_irq_kill_zcmt
tests.description: IRQ KILL   - IRQ Abort/Restart of Zcmt JT/JALT UOP Sequences

tests.name: trap_excp_basic
tests.description: EXCP BASIC - Synchronous Exceptions (Illegal, Misaligned, Access Fault)

tests.name: trap_excp_inst_addr
tests.description: EXCP INST ADDR - Instruction Address Misaligned (MCAUSE=0, requires no C extension)

tests.name: trap_no_c_misencoded
tests.description: NO-C MISENC - misa.C-adaptive: C=1 valid-RVC no-trap regression guard / C=0 bits[1:0]!=0b11 -> illegal mcause=2

tests.name: trap_excp_priv_modes
tests.description: EXCP PRIV  - Privilege Mode Transitions (M/S/U modes, delegation, CSR access)

tests.name: trap_excp_priority
tests.description: EXCP PRIO  - Exception Priority (misalignment in EX wins over access fault in WB)

tests.name: trap_excp_cross_stage_priority
tests.description: EXCP XPRIO - Cross-stage WB-loadacf x EX-illegal: older (WB) cause must win, not younger (EX)

tests.name: trap_excp_nested
tests.description: EXCP NEST  - Nested Exception Handling (exception inside delegated S-mode handler)

tests.name: trap_s_nested_excp
tests.description: EXCP SNEST - Delegable exception inside an S-mode IRQ handler is delegated to S (not forced to M)

tests.name: trap_s_sie_sip_write_mask
tests.description: S WR MASK  - SIE/SIP write-side mideleg masking (Priv 12.1.3): S writes must not leak to non-delegated MIE/MIP bits

tests.name: trap_lockup
tests.description: LOCKUP     - CPU Lockup State (double M-mode exception stalls fetch, lockup_o asserted)

tests.name: trap_excp_ifault
tests.description: EXCP IFAULT- Instruction Fetch Fault (MCAUSE=1, jump to unmapped address)

tests.name: trap_excp_ifault_seq
tests.description: EXCP IFAULT SEQ- Inst access fault via sequential fall-off past SRAM_X (MCAUSE=1, MEPC=MTVAL exact PC)

tests.name: trap_excp_ifault_straddle
tests.description: EXCP IFAULT STRADDLE - 32-bit instr straddles SRAM_X/unmapped boundary; mcause=1, mepc=A=0x8000FFFE, mtval=A+2=0x80010000 (incomplete head parcel must not block deferred IAF)

tests.name: trap_excp_ifault_mispred
tests.description: EXCP IFAULT MISPRED - not-taken cond branch (speculatively taken then cancelled) just upstream of a sequential IAF; the speculate/cancel must NOT drop the pending fault (mcause=1, mepc=mtval=0x80010000)

tests.name: trap_excp_ifault_popret
tests.description: EXCP IFAULT POPRET - CM.POPRET (UOP-final branch, always confirmed) redirects away from an abandoned sequential-prefetch IAF @0x80010000; the abandoned-path fault must be DISCARDED (no spurious IAF at the valid return target). trap_count must stay 0

tests.name: trap_mtval
tests.description: TRAP MTVAL - MTVAL Register Verification (misaligned, access fault, illegal inst)

tests.name: trap_tsr_tw
tests.description: TRAP TSR/TW- MSTATUS.TSR and MSTATUS.TW Bit Verification

tests.name: trap_drain
tests.description: TRAP DRAIN - Pipeline Drain Verification During Trap Entry

tests.name: trap_mepc_align
tests.description: TRAP MEPC  - MEPC Alignment Verification (bit 0 always 0)

tests.name: trap_stval
tests.description: TRAP STVAL - STVAL Register for Delegated S-mode Exceptions

tests.name: trap_mret_edge
tests.description: TRAP MRET  - MRET/SRET Edge Cases (MPIE=0, SPIE=0, double MRET)

tests.name: trap_hazard_ldst
tests.description: TRAP HAZARD- Load/Store Pipeline Hazard Stress Across Trap Boundaries

tests.name: trap_wfi_umode
tests.description: WFI UMODE  - WFI Behavior in U-mode (TW=1 traps, TW=0 stalls)

tests.name: trap_vectored_deleg
tests.description: VEC DELEG  - Combined MTVEC+STVEC Vectored Mode with Delegation

tests.name: trap_sip_readonly
tests.description: SIP RDONLY - SIP.STIP and SIP.SEIP Read-Only Verification

tests.name: trap_csr_reserved
tests.description: CSR RSVD   - Reserved CSR Field Handling (MTVEC/STVEC MODE, MPP)

tests.name: trap_csr_illegal_rd
tests.description: CSR ILLRD  - Illegal CSR access must not write rd (spec: faulting CSR inst shall not retire)

tests.name: trap_priv_sret_illegal
tests.description: SU_MODE PRIV - SRET raises illegal-instruction when SU_MODE_EN=0

tests.name: trap_priv_sfence_illegal
tests.description: SU_MODE PRIV - SFENCE.VMA raises illegal-instruction when SU_MODE_EN=0

tests.name: trap_priv_smode_csrs_razwi
tests.description: SU_MODE PRIV - S-mode shadow CSRs RAZ/WI + misa[S]=misa[U]=0 when SU_MODE_EN=0

tests.name: trap_priv_mideleg_razwi
tests.description: SU_MODE PRIV - mideleg/medeleg RAZ/WI when SU_MODE_EN=0

tests.name: trap_priv_mpp_forced_m
tests.description: SU_MODE PRIV - mstatus.MPP hardwired to M (2'b11) when SU_MODE_EN=0

tests.name: trap_priv_no_delegation
tests.description: SU_MODE PRIV - delegation inert: IRQ enters M-mode handler when SU_MODE_EN=0

tests.name: trap_m_illegal_nom
tests.description: M ILL NOM  - MUL/DIV must trap illegal when M_EXTENSION==0

tests.name: trap_m_illegal_zmmul
tests.description: M ILL ZMM  - DIV/REM must trap illegal under Zmmul

tests.name: trap_lsu_waw_kill
tests.description: WAW PROBE  - LSU WAW vs trap_kill_ex must not suppress in-flight load (P2 C1 probe)

tests.name: trap_zcmt_jt_fault
tests.description: ZCMT FAULT - cm.jt/cm.jalt JVT load fault must trap (no livelock)

tests.name: trap_nmi_kill_muldiv
tests.description: NMI KILLMD - NMI must abort multi-cycle DIV even when irqkill_cfg=0

tests.name: inst_csr_mstatus_warl
tests.description: MSTATUS WL - SUM/MXR/TVM must be WARL writable (S-mode advertised)

tests.name: trap_nmi_post_mnret_irq
tests.description: NMI POSTMR - irq_suppress_post_mret must arm on mnret_taken

tests.name: trap_smrnmi_mnret_raw
tests.description: MNRET RAW  - back-to-back csrw mnepc; mnret must read NEW mnepc (xret stall)

tests.name: trap_smrnmi_mprv
tests.description: SMRNMI MPRV- RNMI-handler store must be M-tagged when NMIE=0 (MPRV ignored per RISC-V Priv spec); bug-sensitive NMIE-gate reproducer

tests.name: trap_smrnmi_excp_preempt
tests.description: SMRNMI DEV - accepted-deviation lock: NMI preempts in-flight posted store -> store-fault dropped, store not replayed

tests.name: trap_zcmp_pop_fault
tests.description: ZCMP FAULT - cm.pop sync access-fault must abort sequencer (no re-trap, no pollution)

tests.name: trap_zcmp_popret_partial_atomic
tests.description: ZCMP ATOMIC - cm.popret SP-update must not commit if trailing load faults (Unpriv 28.13.4.2)

tests.name: trap_excp_ldst_reserved
tests.description: LDST RSV   - LOAD/STORE reserved funct3 (RV64 LD/LWU/SD) must raise illegal-inst (mcause=2)

tests.name: trap_excp_mret_priv
tests.description: MRET PRIV  - MRET from S/U-mode must raise illegal-inst (mcause=2), not return/escalate

tests.name: trap_csr_mcycle_priv
tests.description: MCY UPRIV  - U-mode reads of mcycle/minstret/mcycleh must trap illegal (mcause=2)

tests.name: inst_csr_smode_warl
tests.description: S-WARL     - scounteren / senvcfg / menvcfg / menvcfgh / satp WARL + SIE mideleg mask

tests.name: inst_zca_slli_hint
tests.description: C.SLLI HINT - c.slli x0,X must NOT trap (RVC HINT space)

tests.name: trap_mprv
tests.description: MPRV       - MSTATUS.MPRV Load/Store Privilege Override Verification

tests.name: inst_rv32e_basic
tests.description: RV32E BASIC- RV32E sanity/template: ALU/load-store/branch using only x0-x15, x15(a5) sync sentinel (STD+COMP=RV32EC)

tests.name: inst_rv32e_xregs
tests.description: RV32E XREGS- contract: x16-x31 read-0/write-dropped/NO illegal-inst trap (reserved=>UNSPECIFIED, spec-permitted)

tests.name: coremark
tests.description: CoreMark CPU benchmark

tests.name: dhrystone_v2.1
tests.description: Dhrystone 2.1 benchmark

tests.name: dhrystone_4mcu
tests.description: Dhrystone for MCU benchmark

tests.name: embench_aha-mont64
tests.description: Performs 64-bit Montgomery multiplication, a core operation in public-key cryptography (like RSA).

tests.name: embench_crc32
tests.description: 32-bit Cyclic Redundancy Check; tests bitwise XOR and shifting performance

tests.name: embench_cubic
tests.description: Solves cubic equations; tests floating-point or fixed-point performance and branching.

tests.name: embench_edn
tests.description: Tests the conversion and processing of EDN (Extensible Data Notation) formats.

tests.name: embench_huffbench
tests.description: Huffman decoding, a fundamental algorithm for data decompression (e.g., Gzip/Deflate).

tests.name: embench_matmult-int
tests.description: Standard integer matrix multiplication, measuring row/column traversal efficiency.

tests.name: embench_minver
tests.description: Matrix inversion, used frequently in control systems and robotics.

tests.name: embench_md5sum
tests.description: Implements the MD5 message-digest algorithm, common in legacy integrity checking.

tests.name: embench_nbody
tests.description: Simulates the motion of particles under physical forces; tests floating-point logic.

tests.name: embench_nettle-aes
tests.description: Measures performance of the Advanced Encryption Standard (AES) algorithm using the Nettle library.

tests.name: embench_nettle-sha256
tests.description: Tests SHA-256 hashing, critical for secure boot and data integrity.

tests.name: embench_nsichneu
tests.description: Simulates a large, complex Petri-net (automata); extremely branch-intensive.

tests.name: embench_picojpeg
tests.description: A tiny JPEG decompressor; tests data stream handling and complex nested branching.

tests.name: embench_primecount
tests.description: Counts prime numbers using a sieve; tests bit manipulation and memory access patterns.

tests.name: embench_qrduino
tests.description: A port of the QR code generator; involves complex data encoding logic.

tests.name: embench_sglib-combined
tests.description: Uses the SGLib library to perform various data structure operations (linked lists, trees).

tests.name: embench_slre
tests.description: Super Light Regular Expression; tests recursive parsing and string matching.

tests.name: embench_st
tests.description: Statistics benchmark; calculates basic statistical measures (mean, variance) on a dataset.

tests.name: embench_statemate
tests.description: A state-machine execution test derived from high-level model code generators.

tests.name: embench_tarfind
tests.description: Simulates searching through a TAR archive; tests string comparison and file-structure parsing.

tests.name: embench_ud
tests.description: LU Decomposition (Lower-Upper), a staple linear algebra algorithm for solving simultaneous equations.

tests.name: embench_wikisort
tests.description: A high-performance merge sort implementation; tests array manipulation and comparison logic.
